questions.q: The first thing a Mac user should run before the Hermes installer is:
questions.explanation: `xcode-select --install` provides the build tools the installer needs. Skip it and the installer fails with a misleading error.

questions.q: When the VPS installer asks 'Import from OpenClaw?', a first-time VPS user should answer:
options: No — fresh install is more reliable
questions.explanation: Per the source video, OpenClaw import is buggy. Cron jobs do not migrate cleanly. Fresh install is safer.

questions.q: Short answer: What is the single most important day-one habit in your model provider's dashboard?
questions.explanation: Set a hard monthly budget cap. Without it, a 3am runaway session burns real money in minutes.

questions.q: Which model should a beginner pick if cost matters most?
options: MiniMax M3 / Kimi K2.6 / DeepSeek V4 Pro
questions.explanation: Cheap-tier models (Kimi, DeepSeek, MiniMax) cost $5–$10/month at beginner volume. Opus runs 4–5× that.

questions.q: You're on a Windows PC. Which install path should you pick?
questions.explanation: Local Windows install is rougher than Mac. VPS gives every user the same Linux install via SSH.

questions.q: Which three commands prove Hermes is installed and healthy?
questions.explanation: `hermes doctor` (env checks), `hermes status` (config + keys), `hermes skills list` (invocables). All three return cleanly when verified.
